items.title: Overview
items.description: MCP Client integration for Health Assistant, the self-hosted, open-source health records platform. Connect any Model Context Protocol (MCP) server and expose its tools to the AI clinical assistant over STDIO, HTTP, or SSE transports.

items.title: STDIO Server Setup
items.description: Set up a STDIO MCP server in Health Assistant. Run a Model Context Protocol server as a local subprocess with an admin command allowlist, encrypted environment variables, and per-process concurrency limits.

items.title: HTTP / SSE Server Setup
items.description: Set up an HTTP or SSE MCP server connection in Health Assistant. Connect to a remote Model Context Protocol server with bearer auth, custom headers, and TLS certificate verification.

items.title: Environment Variables
items.description: Environment variables for the MCP Client integration in Health Assistant. Configure INTEGRATION_SECRET_KEY for field encryption, the STDIO command allowlist, the insecure-HTTP toggle, and resource limits.

items.title: Security Model
items.description: Security model for the MCP Client integration in Health Assistant. Mitigations for arbitrary code execution, secret encryption at rest, prompt injection, DoS, and transport MITM across STDIO and HTTP/SSE transports.

items.title: Troubleshooting
items.description: Troubleshooting the MCP Client integration in Health Assistant. Resolve STDIO allowlist errors, a missing INTEGRATION_SECRET_KEY, connection failures, and tools not appearing in the AI chat assistant.
